title: Scancell to go public after winning reverse merger bid for troubled Neuphoria
summary: Scancell and Neuphoria have made their union official via a reverse merger that will result in a Nasdaq listing for Scancell and a resolution for Neuphoria’s shareholders after the biotech ended its social anxiety program last year.

title: Lilly’s triple-G drug hits 22.6% weight loss, but impact on reducing cardio risk is less clear
summary: Eli Lilly’s triple-G drug retatrutide has hit the primary endpoints of a pair of phase 3 obesity trials, although the peak weight loss fell short of previous trials while failing to prove the drug reduces cardiovascular risk.

title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Clinuvel cuts fifth of staff; Novartis loses 322 workers
summary: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

title: FDA clears Johnson & Johnson’s surgical robot for ‘the next era in surgery’
summary: The FDA has authorized Johnson & Johnson’s Ottava Robotic Surgical System as the company looks to compete with Intuitive Surgical and Medtronic.

title: Roche Diagnostics growth remains muted amid China headwinds
summary: Roche’s Diagnostics division continues to battle growth headwinds, notching a 3% increase in sales.

title: Australian biopharma Clinuvel to shed up to 20% of workforce in move to US
summary: Australian biopharma Clinuvel Pharmaceuticals is moving its headquarters to the U.S.— but up to a fifth of the company’s workforce won’t be staying for the trans-Pacific pivot.

title: ‘Substantially better than before’: Why India has golden opportunity to build on innovative R&D wins
summary: A string of recent wins on the frontiers of novel drug development could mean that a homegrown innovation industry is within reach.

title: Roche drops TCE for lupus, but hasn’t given up on autoimmune cell therapies
summary: Roche may have scrapped work on a T-cell engager being evaluated for lupus, but the company’s head of pharma told Fierce that still has a strategy for developing cell therapies for autoimmune diseases.

title: NIH joins Trump’s $5B AI mission with biomedical research push
summary: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services announced new efforts Wednesday aligned with President Donald Trump’s artificial intelligence-driven Genesis Mission. The centerpiece of HHS’ participation is the NIH-led Bio Genesis Mission

title: House passes bill directing FDA to update animal testing rules
summary: The U.S. House of Representatives passed the FDA Modernization Act 3.0 this week, and lawmakers and animal welfare advocates are celebrating the bill as a step toward a new era in preclinical testing and drug development.

title: Royalty Pharma pens $425M deal for slice of sales on AstraZeneca’s ATTR drug
summary: Royalty Pharma has secured a stake in another transthyretin-mediated amyloidosis drug, this time paying $125 million upfront to Neurimmune for a cut of the sales of its AstraZeneca-partnered med.

title: Biotech industry leaders file brief backing FDA against ‘malicious’ mifepristone challenge
summary: A group of 61 biopharma companies, executives and investors has filed an amicus brief in support of the FDA in an ongoing case brought by Louisiana meant to remove access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: Crystalys mines $130M series B to push gout drug through phase 3
summary: Crystalys Therapeutics has raised $130 million in series B financing to advance the development of its gout drug dotinurad and prepare for a potential commercial launch.

title: Celldex ends skin disease program after failing phase 2 test
summary: A phase 2 study of Celldex’s barzolvolimab in a chronic skin disease has missed its primary endpoint, driving the biotech to end development in the indication and raising doubts about an upcoming eczema readout.

title: ‘We’re moving quickly’: Startup bags rare disease drug as Thiel-backed CEO eyes $50M fundraise
summary: A rare disease startup with a mission to repurpose proven drugs has licensed a Chinese formulation of a well-known medicine and set its sights on a $50 million fundraise.

title: Roche defends 6B franc Vabysmo peak sales target despite another quarterly miss
summary: On an investor call Thursday, Roche Pharmaceuticals CEO Teresa Graham assured analysts that the U.S. branded market has stabilized after a period of contraction, though the explosive growth that Vabysmo enjoyed early in its launch is likely behind it.

title: BeOne jumps aboard onshoring train with $300M to add small molecule production in US
summary: As the recent reshoring zeal rolls on in the United States, BeOne Medicines is beefing up its flagship manufacturing and R&D campus in New Jersey to the tune of $300 million.

title: Regulatory tracker: AZ gains EU nod for camizestrant; China 1st to endorse Takeda's narcolepsy drug
summary: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for standalone stories, but we think they are still worth mentioning.

title: FDA rebukes 2 Indian API plants over data controls, sanitation issues
summary: The FDA slammed a pair of India-based API manufacturers in separate warning letters outlining poor record keeping, problems maintaining sanitation and licensing issues, among other observations.

title: J&J’s bispecific combo slashes myeloma progression risk by 89% in phase 3 trial
summary: Johnson & Johnson has unveiled remarkable phase 3 survival data for its dual bispecific combination in earlier-line multiple myeloma, potentially adding a new twist in the commercial trajectories of off-the-shelf T-cell engagers versus personalized CAR-T therapies.

title: Australian biopharma Clinuvel to shed up to 20% of workforce in move to US

summary: A string of recent wins on the frontiers of novel drug development could mean that a homegrown innovation industry is within reach.

title: ARS Pharma drafts NFL legend Drew Brees in allergy awareness push
summary: The former quarterback spoke with Fierce Pharma Marketing on teaming up with ARS, his journey with food allergies and why needle-free epinephrine Neffy is an "incredible option."

title: GSK secures first lung cancer approval as FDA clears Jideytro in quick payoff from Nuvalent deal
summary: In a landmark moment for its oncology ambitions, GSK has secured FDA approval for Jideytro as the first commercial lung cancer drug in the company’s history. The milestone was made possible by GSK’s recent acquisition of Nuvalent for $10.6 billion.

title: FDA calls out Sanofi emails for giving 'misleading impression' of Beyfortus use in RSV
summary: The FDA's Office of Prescription Drug Promotion took issue with Sanofi's characterization of antibody Beyfortus as an RSV preventative, as the med is specifically indicated for RSV lower respiratory tract disease.

title: What to Expect at PODD 2026
summary: Directors Andrew Goldstein and Valerie Bowling discuss what to expect at the 2026 PODD: Partnership Opportunities in Drug Delivery conference.

title: Trump floats countdown to 200% tariffs on generic drugs imported to US
summary: While the White House focused its initial Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs on branded drugs, generics makers—who supply the vast majority of U.S. prescriptions—won’t stay in the clear for long.

title: Summit Therapeutics updates ivonescimab survival data ahead of FDA decision date
summary: Aiming to bolster ivonescimab’s case ahead of a key FDA decision, Summit Therapeutics has provided updated overall survival data from the phase 3 HARMONi trial for its PD-1xVEGF bispecific antibody.

title: Look out Ionis, here comes Arrowhead with promising hypertriglyceridemia data
summary: Less than a month after Ionis scored an FDA expansion for Tryngolza to become the first drug to specifically treat severe hypertriglyceridemia, Arrowhead has countered with head-turning data from two phase 3 trials that could set the company up for a matching nod.

summary: The Lantus Solostar insulin pen isn't in official FDA shortage, but patients are having a tough time finding it.

summary: Too few senators were in attendance to proceed with a vote on the CDC director nominee.

summary: Congress is scrambling to advance a slew of health care bills before an August recess. Price transparency and prior authorization top the list.
